description: Asthma is a chronic inflammatory disease of the airways characterized by variable airflow obstruction, bronchial hyperresponsiveness, and underlying inflammation. Symptoms are often triggered by allergens, infections, or exercise.

epidemiology.prevalence: Very common; affects children and adults globally
risk_factors: Family history of atopy or asthma

symptoms: Symptom variability with triggers or time of day

clinical: Symptoms triggered by cold air, exercise, allergens
investigations: Spirometry: FEV1/FVC reduced and reversible with bronchodilator
Exclude differentials: COPD, vocal cord dysfunction, heart failure

maintenance: Long-acting beta-agonists (LABA) if ICS alone is insufficient
acute_exacerbation: Oral corticosteroids (e.g. prednisolone 40mg PO for 5 days)

contraindications.NSAIDs: May trigger attacks in sensitive patients

contextual_notes.triage_alert: PEFR <33% or inability to complete sentences = severe exacerbation requiring urgent intervention
contextual_notes.low_resource_setting: Ensure access to salbutamol inhalers and basic peak flow monitoring
